title: ETZ-Swap and KYCnotlist: Why This Partnership Matters for Users Who Want Clearer No-KYC Exchange Choices

friendlyUrls.anchor: WellCrypto for a safer, faster swap route
MoneroDEX for comparing XMR swap routes

keyQuestions: What is KYCnotlist in simple terms?
Why do privacy-focused users need a discovery layer like KYCnotlist?
Why does ETZ-Swap fit naturally on KYCnotlist?
What do ETZ-Swap users gain from this partnership?
Why is this more useful than a generic listing?
Does KYCnotlist replace checking the final exchange details yourself?
quickSteps: Start with KYCnotlist to narrow the field and compare relevant exchangers by direction, visible labels, and practical conditions.
Treat KYCnotlist as a discovery layer, not the final decision, and still review the exchanger itself before sending funds.
Use ETZ-Swap when the route and conditions fit your goal, and make sure the path matches your privacy and speed priorities.
Compare context, not just the headline rate, because the most attractive number is not always the most practical route.
Keep the process simple and deliberate by using trusted discovery, verifying the final details, and choosing the exchanger that fits the real task.

recoveryDecisionTree.start: You found ETZ-Swap through KYCnotlist and want to choose the route carefully

then: Use KYCnotlist to narrow the field by direction and basic conditions
Open only the exchangers that match your actual route
Compare practical fit, not just the first visible rate

branches.if: You already decided to use ETZ-Swap
then: Open ETZ-Swap directly from the comparison context
Review the route, limits, and exchange details on the service itself
Make sure the path matches your privacy and speed priorities before proceeding

branches.if: The listing helped discovery but you still feel uncertain
then: Treat the monitor as a starting point, not the final guarantee
Verify the final exchange details yourself before sending funds
Choose the exchanger that makes sense for your actual task instead of following noise

riskNotes: KYCnotlist improves discovery and comparison, but it does not execute the swap for the user.
A focused monitor reduces noise, but users should still verify the exchanger itself before sending funds.
Better discovery is about context and fit, not only about finding the highest headline rate.
Relevant traffic from a niche comparison environment is usually more useful than broad untargeted exposure.
The strongest value of this partnership is qualified discovery inside the right user context.
